children.heading: Establishment and scope of program
children.label: a Establishment and scope of program
A
children.text: an identification and classification according to eutrophic condition of all publicly owned lakes in such State;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of procedures, processes, and methods (including land use requirements), to control sources of pollution of such lakes;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of methods and procedures, in conjunction with appropriate Federal agencies, to restore the quality of such lakes;

children.label: D
children.text: methods and procedures to mitigate the harmful effects of high acidity, including innovative methods of neutralizing and restoring buffering capacity of lakes and methods of removing from lakes toxic metals and other toxic substances mobilized by high acidity;

children.label: E
children.text: a list and description of those publicly owned lakes in such State for which uses are known to be impaired, including those lakes which are known not to meet applicable water quality standards or which require implementation of control programs to maintain compliance with applicable standards and those lakes in which water quality has deteriorated as a result of high acidity that may reasonably be due to acid deposition; and

children.label: F
children.text: an assessment of the status and trends of water quality in lakes in such State, including but not limited to, the nature and extent of pollution loading from point and nonpoint sources and the extent to which the use of lakes is impaired as a result of such pollution, particularly with respect to toxic pollution.

children.heading: Submission as part of 1315(b)(1) report
children.label: 2 Submission as part of 1315(b)(1) report
children.text: The information required under paragraph (1) shall be included in the report required under , beginning with the report required under such section by .section 1315(b)(1) of this titleApril 1, 1988

children.heading: Report of Administrator
children.label: 3 Report of Administrator
children.text: Not later than 180 days after receipt from the States of the biennial information required under paragraph (1), the Administrator shall submit to the Committee on Public Works and Transportation of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate a report on the status of water quality in lakes in the United States, including the effectiveness of the methods and procedures described in paragraph (1)(D).

children.heading: Eligibility requirement
children.label: 4 Eligibility requirement
children.text: Beginning after , a State must have submitted the information required under paragraph (1) in order to receive grant assistance under this section.April 1, 1988

children.heading: Financial assistance to States
children.label: b Financial assistance to States
children.text: The Administrator shall provide financial assistance to States in order to carry out methods and procedures approved by him under subsection (a) of this section. The Administrator shall provide financial assistance to States to prepare the identification and classification surveys required in subsection (a)(1) of this section.

children.heading: Maximum amount of grant; authorization of appropriations
children.label: c Maximum amount of grant; authorization of appropriations

children.text: The amount granted to any State for any fiscal year under subsection (b) of this section shall not exceed 70 per centum of the funds expended by such State in such year for carrying out approved methods and procedures under subsection (a) of this section.

There is authorized to be appropriated $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2001 through 2005 for grants to States under subsection (b) of this section which such sums shall remain available until expended. The Administrator shall provide for an equitable distribution of such sums to the States with approved methods and procedures under subsection (a) of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: develop cost effective technologies for the control of pollutants to preserve or enhance lake water quality while optimizing multiple lakes uses;

children.label: B
children.text: control nonpoint sources of pollution which are contributing to the degradation of water quality in lakes;

children.label: C
children.text: evaluate the feasibility of implementing regional consolidated pollution control strategies;

children.label: D
children.text: demonstrate environmentally preferred techniques for the removal and disposal of contaminated lake sediments;

children.label: E
children.text: develop improved methods for the removal of silt, stumps, aquatic growth, and other obstructions which impair the quality of lakes;

children.label: F
children.text: construct and evaluate silt traps and other devices or equipment to prevent or abate the deposit of sediment in lakes; and

children.label: G
children.text: demonstrate the costs and benefits of utilizing dredged material from lakes in the reclamation of despoiled land.

children.heading: Geographical requirements
children.label: 2 Geographical requirements
children.text: Demonstration projects authorized by this subsection shall be undertaken to reflect a variety of geographical and environmental conditions. As a priority, the Administrator shall undertake demonstration projects at Lake Champlain, New York and Vermont; Lake Houston, Texas; Beaver Lake, Arkansas; Greenwood Lake and Belcher Creek, New Jersey; Deal Lake, New Jersey; Alcyon Lake, New Jersey; Gorton’s Pond, Rhode Island; Lake Washington, Rhode Island; Lake Bomoseen, Vermont; Sauk Lake, Minnesota; Otsego Lake, New York; Oneida Lake, New York; Raystown Lake, Pennsylvania; Swan Lake, Itasca County, Minnesota; Walker Lake, Nevada; Lake Tahoe, California and Nevada; Ten Mile Lakes, Oregon; Woahink Lake, Oregon; Highland Lake, Connecticut; Lily Lake, New Jersey; Strawbridge Lake, New Jersey; Baboosic Lake, New Hampshire; French Pond, New Hampshire; Dillon Reservoir, Ohio; Tohopekaliga Lake, Florida; Lake Apopka, Florida; Lake George, New York; Lake Wallenpaupack, Pennsylvania; Lake Allatoona, Georgia; and Lake Worth, Texas.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 3 Reports
children.text: Notwithstanding section 3003 of the Federal Reports Elimination and Sunset Act of 1995 ( note; ), by , and January 1 of every odd-numbered year thereafter, the Administrator shall report to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate on work undertaken pursuant to this subsection. Upon completion of the program authorized by this subsection, the Administrator shall submit to such committees a final report on the results of such program, along with recommendations for further measures to improve the water quality of the Nation’s lakes.31 U.S.C. 1113109 Stat. 734–736January 1, 1997

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection not to exceed $40,000,000 for fiscal years beginning after , to remain available until expended.September 30, 1986

children.heading: Amount
children.label: i Amount
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out subsection (b) with respect to subsection (a)(1)(D) not to exceed $25,000,000 for fiscal years beginning after , to remain available until expended.September 30, 1986

children.heading: Distribution of funds
children.label: ii Distribution of funds
children.text: The Administrator shall provide for an equitable distribution of sums appropriated pursuant to this subparagraph among States carrying out approved methods and procedures. Such distribution shall be based on the relative needs of each such State for the mitigation of the harmful effects on lakes and other surface waters of high acidity that may reasonably be due to acid deposition or acid mine drainage.

children.heading: Grants as additional assistance
children.label: iii Grants as additional assistance
children.text: The amount of any grant to a State under this subparagraph shall be in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other Federal financial assistance.
